author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session focused on backfilling 15 historical Claude Code session transcripts into the ameen-context MCP structured memory system. The work involved inspecting the JSONL format of raw session queue files, discovering that entries contain a `cwd` field usable for project classification, sampling one file from each of the 15 project directories to extract their actual working directory paths, updating the project registry (registry.json) to add missing project mappings for DHS Consulting and IDeaS-Lab paths, and writing a v2 LLM structuring pass script (`hooks/structure_sessions.py`) plus a backfill script to process the historical sessions.
problems: 15 historical session transcript files across different project directories needed to be backfilled into the structured memory system
Project registry was missing mappings for DHS Consulting and IDeaS-Lab working directory paths
JSONL format of raw session queue files needed to be verified before building the structuring pass
approaches: Sampled JSONL entries to discover schema — entries have keys: type, operation, timestamp, sessionId, content, and cwd
Extracted cwd field from one file per project directory to get the canonical working directory for each session
Updated registry.json with two new entries to cover previously unmapped project paths
Wrote hooks/structure_sessions.py as the v2 LLM-based structuring pass for raw session queue entries
Wrote a backfill script to process the 15 historical sessions through the structuring pipeline
results: Confirmed JSONL entries contain a `cwd` field enabling reliable project classification without LLM inference
Identified all 15 project directory paths and their canonical cwds
registry.json updated successfully with DHS Consulting and IDeaS-Lab path mappings
Backfill script created to process historical sessions

decisions.decision: Use cwd field from JSONL entries as primary signal for project classification
Update registry.json to include DHS Consulting and IDeaS-Lab project paths
Build backfill script to process 15 historical sessions through the v2 structuring pipeline

open_questions: Has the backfill script been run against all 15 historical sessions?
Did the structuring pass produce valid structured output for the historical sessions?
Are there any project directories that still lack registry entries after the two additions?
